landing.heroSubheading: Trainers build plans. Trainees log workouts. In under a minute.
landing.feature1Description: Create multi-week programs with sets, reps, and weights.
landing.feature2Description: See last week's results inline and record today's sets fast.
landing.feature3Description: Trainers monitor every session. Trainees watch their lifts grow.
landing.footerLogin: Already have an account? Log in
landing.demoSubtitle: Explore Forge without signing up — log in as a demo account

help.heading: Help & FAQ

trainers.q1: How do I create a workout plan?
trainers.a1: Go to the Plans tab and click 'New Plan'. Give your plan a name and set the number of weeks. Then add workout schemas (like 'Push Day' or 'Pull Day') to each week. Inside each schema, add exercises from your library with the target sets, reps, and weight.
trainers.q2: How do I assign a plan to a trainee?
trainers.a2: Open any plan and click 'Assign'. Select a trainee from your connected roster. Review the exercise weights and adjust if needed for that specific trainee. Once assigned, the trainee will see their scheduled workouts immediately.
trainers.q3: How do I view a trainee's progress?
trainers.a3: Go to the Trainees tab and click on any trainee's name. You'll see their workout history with every session, exercise, and the sets, reps, and weights they actually logged. Use the Exercises tab to see cross-plan progress charts for individual exercises.

trainees.q1: How do I join a trainer?
trainees.a1: Your trainer will send you an invite link or code. Click the link or enter the code on the join page. Once connected, you'll see the workout plans your trainer assigns to you.
trainees.q2: How do I log a workout?
trainees.a2: Open your current plan from the Plans tab. Tap the workout scheduled for today to start a session. For each exercise, you'll see last week's results inline. Enter your actual weight, reps, and sets for each exercise. Tap 'Finish Workout' when you're done.
trainees.q3: How do I track my body weight?
trainees.a3: Go to your Profile tab to log your body weight. Enter today's weight and confirm. Your trainer can see your body weight history once you grant them access — you'll be prompted to approve this the first time they request it.

help.guideLink: New here? Read the Getting Started guide →

trainers.intro: Follow these steps to set up your first training programme.
trainers.step1Body: Go to the Exercises tab and add the exercises you want to use. Give each one a name and optionally a category. These build your personal exercise library.
trainers.step2Body: Go to the Plans tab and click 'New Plan'. Name your plan and set the number of weeks. Add workout schemas (e.g. 'Push Day', 'Pull Day') to each week, then add exercises from your library to each schema with target sets, reps, and weight.
trainers.step3Title: Assign the plan to a trainee
trainers.step3Body: Open the plan and click 'Assign'. Select a trainee from your connected roster. Review and adjust the exercise weights for that specific trainee, then confirm.
trainers.step4Body: Go to the Trainees tab and click a trainee's name. You'll see their full session history with the actual reps and weights they logged. Use the Exercises tab to view cross-plan progress charts for individual exercises.

trainees.intro: Follow these steps to get started with your training.
trainees.step1Body: Your trainer will send you an invite link. Click the link to connect your account to theirs. Once connected, you'll see the workout plans they assign to you.
trainees.step2Body: Open your current plan from the Plans tab. Tap the workout scheduled for today to start a session. For each exercise, you'll see last week's results inline. Enter your actual weight, reps, and sets, then tap 'Finish Workout' when done.
trainees.step3Body: Go to the Exercises tab to see progress charts for every exercise across all your plans. You can also open any completed plan to browse session history week by week.

join.invalidBody: This invite link is invalid or has been revoked. Ask your trainer to send a new one.
join.alreadyConnectedBody: You are already connected to a trainer. You must disconnect from your current trainer before joining a new one.
join.connectionFailedBody: Something went wrong while connecting you to your trainer. Please try the link again.

completedPlan.tapExercise: Tap an exercise to view progress chart.

fixed.label: Fixed
fixed.description: Weight and reps stay the same every session. Use for technique work, warm-ups, or when you want full manual control.

linear.label: Linear
linear.description: Add a fixed amount of weight each session (e.g. +2.5 kg). Best for beginners where strength increases every workout.

doubleProgression.label: Double progression
doubleProgression.description: Progress reps first within a target range (e.g. 3×8–12), then increase weight and reset to the bottom of the range. Good for intermediate trainees.

rpe.label: RPE
rpe.description: Rate of Perceived Exertion (1–10). Trainer sets a target RPE (e.g. RPE 8 = 2 reps left in the tank). Load adjusts session to session based on daily readiness.

rir.label: RIR
rir.description: Reps In Reserve — how many reps you could still do before failure. RIR 2 means stop when 2 reps remain. Similar to RPE but often more intuitive.
